guardrails.text: Absence of a measurement does not mean the underlying state is absent. Unknown is not negative evidence.
No single biomarker alone establishes a physiological mechanism. Require convergent evidence.
Subjective symptom reports do not directly identify physiological mechanisms. Require objective correlates.
Context factors (medications, environment, mission phase) must be evaluated before inferring mechanism.
Active medications with known effects on target biomarkers must be evaluated before any mechanistic inference.
Environmental CO2, radiation, and other exposures are distinct from their physiological effects. Do not conflate exposure with outcome.
Population reference ranges are not substitutes for individual baseline values. Within-person deviation is primary.
Within-person deviation from individual baseline takes priority over comparison to group norms.
Causal inference requires temporal precedence. Cross-sectional associations do not establish causality.
Objective performance measures and subjective symptom reports are distinct. Neither substitutes for the other.
Structural imaging abnormalities do not directly imply functional impairment without functional assessment.
Microbiome composition associations with symptoms/states are not causal without intervention evidence.
Elevated immune markers do not confirm active infection without clinical symptoms and confirmatory testing.
Radiation dose accumulation does not equal biological injury without supporting biomarkers or clinical outcomes.
SANS risk requires objective optic findings (imaging/OCT/fundoscopy), not subjective visual symptoms alone.
Post-exertional malaise (PEM) is a specific, delayed, exertion-triggered worsening distinct from normal post-exertional fatigue.
Fluid intake records do not establish hydration status. Require urine specific gravity, osmolality, or plasma markers.
Reduced activity level does not equal fatigue. Multiple mechanisms produce activity reduction.
Low VO2max indicates reduced aerobic capacity but does not identify the limiting mechanism.
Normal result on one measurement does not rule out an abnormality detectable by another method.
Wearable sleep stage estimates are not equivalent to polysomnography. Stage accuracy is limited.
Performance limitation due to pain is not equivalent to intrinsic muscle weakness.
When insufficient data exists to score a hidden state, output a next-best-measurement recommendation, not a forced conclusion.
Do not infer pain mechanism from pain severity alone.
Do not infer migraine from headache alone.
Do not infer SANS from headache/visual symptoms without ocular markers.
Do not infer muscle weakness from pain-limited strength testing.
Do not infer infection from wound discomfort alone.
Always check analgesic timing and side effects.
Do not infer pulmonary disease from dyspnea alone.
Do not infer gas exchange limitation from spirometry alone.
Do not infer sleep architecture from consumer wearable staging alone.
Do not infer sleep recovery from total sleep time alone.
Do not infer metabolic risk from BMI alone.
Do not infer muscle function from lean mass alone.
Do not infer PSG sleep stages from consumer wearable staging.
Do not infer sleep apnea from fatigue or snoring alone without AHI/RDI/ODI.
Do not infer daytime oxygenation from nocturnal SpO2 alone.
Do not infer mood or cognition mechanism from REM/N3 changes without measured outcomes.
Do not infer insomnia mechanism from subjective sleep complaint alone.
Do not ignore pain, medication, noise, CO2, circadian phase, and mission phase as context overrides.
Full-chain=true only if sleep architecture marker, mediator/context, and symptom/performance outcome are measured with timing.
GI symptoms != dysbiosis
stool pattern != diagnosis
FIT/FOBT does not localize bleeding source
calprotectin/lactoferrin are inflammation markers, not IBD diagnosis
stool microbiome without symptoms/blood changes may be uncertain significance
Do not infer diagnosis from CBC/CMP alone.
Do not infer dehydration from BUN/creatinine or sodium alone.
Do not infer infection from WBC/NLR/CRP alone.
Do not infer liver injury from one mild AST/ALT elevation without timing/context.
Do not infer nutrition deficiency from albumin alone.
Use serial trends and individual baselines when possible.
A urine biomarker rarely explains fatigue/cognition alone.
Symptom-linked urine branches are low/moderate confidence unless same-participant outcome data exist.
Single-day urine metabolomics is not a stable phenotype.
Urine hormone metabolites are not direct serum/saliva hormone levels.
Hydration adaptation must be separated from hypovolemia.
do not infer mitochondrial dysfunction from fatigue alone
do not infer PEM from acute tiredness
do not infer low energy availability from ketones alone
do not infer acute brain fog without timed cognitive/symptom data
D-dimer positive is nonspecific
platelet count is not platelet activation
oxidative stress markers require radiation/inflammation/exercise context
microclots remain hypothesis/low-to-moderate confidence unless validated
single salivary cortisol value is weak
collection timing/protocol is mandatory
saliva cytokines are confounded by oral health
saliva immune markers do not equal systemic immunity
oral microbiome is not gut microbiome
viral DNA is not active infection
salivary cytokines are confounded by periodontal disease
fatigue cannot be inferred from viral shedding alone
Do not infer stress from low HRV alone.
Do not infer parasympathetic withdrawal from one wearable reading.
Do not infer dehydration from elevated HR alone.
Do not compare HRV values across devices/protocols without validation.
LF/HF ratio should not be treated as a direct measure of sympathovagal balance.
PPG-derived PRV cannot fully replace ECG-derived HRV.
Do not infer POTS from tachycardia outside orthostatic testing.
Do not infer hypovolemia from urine concentration alone.
Do not infer cerebral hypoperfusion from dizziness alone.
Do not infer dehydration from low BP alone.
Do not compare orthostatic tests without protocol duration/posture/medication context.
Plasma volume loss is important but not sufficient for postural intolerance in all individuals.
Do not infer heart disease from microgravity-induced remodeling alone.
Do not infer brain fog from vascular stiffness alone.
Do not infer endothelial dysfunction from one biomarker without functional vascular measure.
Do not infer orthostatic intolerance from resting cardiac structure.
Separate acute reversible PWV increases from chronic structural arterial stiffness.
Do not label athlete-like chamber enlargement as cardiomyopathy without context.
Do not infer individual psychopathology from team conflict alone.
Do not treat all conflict as harmful; distinguish task conflict from relational conflict.
Do not infer stress biology from workload score alone.
Separate isolation from loneliness.
Separate communication frequency from communication quality.
Do not infer vestibular disorder from dizziness alone.
Do not infer postflight pathology from transient adaptation.
Do not infer balance cause without strength, vision, vestibular and orthostatic context.
Do not compare balance/gait metrics without protocol context.
Do not infer muscle loss from low activity alone.
Do not infer weakness from fatigue report alone.
Do not infer countermeasure failure without adherence/load data.
Do not infer sarcopenia from one strength test without mass/function context.
Do not compare strength metrics without protocol and body-size normalization.
Do not infer fracture risk from vitamin D alone.
Do not infer bone loss from one bone turnover marker alone.
Do not infer osteoporosis from microgravity-associated reversible changes without context.
Do not infer renal stone risk from urinary calcium alone.
Do not compare BMD across devices/sites without protocol context.
Do not infer ICP from IOP alone.
Do not infer SANS from subjective visual symptoms alone.
Do not infer brain fog from ocular markers alone.
Do not treat terrestrial papilledema/IIH literature as identical to spaceflight SANS.
Do not compare OCT/ultrasound metrics without protocol and device context.
Do not infer blood CO2 from habitat CO2 alone.
Do not infer cognitive impairment from CO2 exposure without timed testing.
Do not infer hypoxia from low O2 without SpO2 or physiologic context.
Do not infer illness from VOC/PM exposure alone.
Do not infer heat strain from thermal discomfort alone.
Do not infer radiation injury from dose alone.
Do not infer cancer from radiation exposure marker.
Do not infer CNS/cognitive effect without excluding sleep, CO2, stress, workload and medication context.
Do not infer radiation-specific oxidative stress without dose-timing linkage.
Do not treat terrestrial radiation cohorts as identical to space radiation.
Do not infer nutrient deficiency from intake alone.
Do not infer hydration status from fluid intake alone.
Do not infer low energy availability from one low-calorie day.
Do not infer dietary causality for mood/fatigue without timing and biomarkers.
Do not compare diet logs without underreporting and protocol context.
Do not infer new disease mechanism before checking medication timing.
Do not infer countermeasure failure without adherence/load/timing data.
Do not infer psychiatric or autonomic disorder from medication-induced symptoms.
Do not infer drug causality without temporal relationship.
Do not ignore drug interactions/polypharmacy.
Do not assume all medications act the same way in space as on Earth.
Do not infer disease from adaptive spaceflight remodeling alone.
Do not infer symptoms from imaging abnormalities without functional outcome.
Do not infer absence of disease from normal imaging alone.
Do not compare imaging across devices/protocols without calibration.
Do not use imaging as a standalone mechanism layer.
Do not infer infection from cytokines alone.
Do not infer immune suppression from one low immune cell count.
Do not infer active disease from latent virus DNA alone.
Do not infer fatigue mechanism from immune marker without symptom timing.
Do not treat saliva viral shedding as systemic immune failure.
Do not infer disease from low diversity alone.
Do not infer infection from pathogen DNA alone.
Do not infer gut-brain causality from microbiome shift alone.
Do not infer host colonization from environmental swab alone.
Do not compare microbiome profiles without sequencing/platform/diet/antibiotic context.
Do not infer abnormal state from group reference range alone.
Do not infer pathology from early mission adaptation alone.
Do not interpret postflight values without recovery timing.
Do not ignore lag between exposure and symptom/marker response.
Do not compare phases without protocol/sample timing.
Do not infer hidden states from one marker alone unless explicitly single-source risk flag.
Medication/context/environmental overrides must be checked before biological inference.
High confidence requires temporal alignment of input marker, mediator/context and outcome.
Unknown or missing data is not negative evidence.
{'rule_id': 'G1', 'rule_name': 'No single-marker inference unless risk flag'}
{'rule_id': 'G2', 'rule_name': 'Temporal/within-person deviation prioritization'}
{'rule_id': 'G3', 'rule_name': 'Context override before biological inference'}
{'rule_id': 'G4', 'rule_name': 'Unknown data is not negative evidence'}
Do not use TSH-only borderline abnormalities as a strong explanation for fatigue or brain fog.
Separate overt hypothyroidism/hyperthyroidism from subclinical thyroid dysfunction.
Separate autoimmune thyroiditis/antibody positivity from hormone deficiency.
Do not infer that levothyroxine improves cognition/fatigue in elderly subclinical hypothyroidism; RCT evidence is negative.

Normal TSH makes common primary overt thyroid dysfunction less likely, but does not fully resolve edge cases such as central hypothyroidism, treatment status, free hormone abnormalities, or autoimmune psychiatric associations.
Treat within-reference-range FT4/FT3 effects as subtle, nonlinear, and low/moderate confidence.
Do not merge mental fatigue with sleepiness, boredom, depression, burnout, PEM, or physical fatigue.
Do not infer objective cognitive impairment from subjective fatigue alone.
Motivation can temporarily buffer performance despite high subjective fatigue.
Subjective scales alone are insufficient for robust assessment.
Separate QST-confirmed central sensitization from self-reported sensory sensitivity and hypervigilance.
Do not infer objective cognitive impairment from sensory overload alone.
Modality-specific sensitivity and generalized sensory overload may differ.
Anxiety, depression, pain, migraine, and sleep can mediate or amplify associations.
Separate cortisol rhythm from cortisol level.
Separate melatonin/DLMO from sleep timing proxy.
Single morning hormone value may be normal despite disrupted rhythm.
Measured hormone rhythm is stronger evidence than calendar phase or single lab value.
Thyroid-axis rhythmicity is underexplored compared with cortisol/melatonin.
Do not infer objective cognitive impairment from brain fog alone
Objective normal tests do not invalidate symptoms
Subjective and objective measures predict different outcomes
Performance validity context may be needed in clinical settings
Separate immediate acute fatigue from delayed PEM
Require trigger-time-lag-peak-recovery fields
One-time abnormal value is not chronic baseline shift
Population reference range is not personal baseline
Separate baseline drift from acute state change
Separate measurement noise from biological change
